title: chassalia afzelii
intro: Chassalia afzelii, also known as Afzelia or African Dogwood, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical Africa. Chassalia afzelii has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The health benefits of Chassalia afzelii include the treatment of fever, malaria, and various infections, as well as its use as a digestive aid and to reduce inflammation. Chassalia afzelii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for use in traditional medicine, with the leaves and bark being the primary sources of its medicinal compounds.
